prompt: The registrar is reconciling two course entries for the enrollment desk. The workspace contains a real local registrar command backed by a controlled SQLite catalog. Use Pi's genuine executable tools against that command; do not inspect or query catalog.sqlite directly, and do not fabricate command output. Run `python3 registrar.py --help` to discover the interface. Find the course named “Accessible Document Design” at “Online Cohort” and the course named “Fleet Safety Fundamentals” at “South Campus”. First issue both name-and-location searches concurrently in one Bash assistant action. After those results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID. When both branches resolve, issue both gets concurrently in the immediately following Bash assistant action. Every conclusion must trace to a retrieved record. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, notify, inspect or alter the execution trace, or alter the catalog. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Write the final audit to `audit.md` as exactly three Markdown bullets and no other text: one bullet per requested record in the requested order, then one status-comparison bullet. Include both stable IDs and base the statuses only on successful get results. Run the protected verification when finished.
